# services.gpg-agent

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.gpg-agent`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `services.gpg-agent.defaultCacheTtl` | `null or signed integer` | Set the time a cache entry is valid to the given number of seconds. |
| `services.gpg-agent.defaultCacheTtlSsh` | `null or signed integer` | Set the time a cache entry used for SSH keys is valid to the given number of seconds. |
| `services.gpg-agent.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable GnuPG private key agent. |
| `services.gpg-agent.enableBashIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Bash integration. |
| `services.gpg-agent.enableExtraSocket` | `boolean` | Whether to enable extra socket of the GnuPG key agent (useful for GPG Agent forwarding). |
| `services.gpg-agent.enableFishIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Fish integration. |
| `services.gpg-agent.enableNushellIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Nushell integration. |
| `services.gpg-agent.enableScDaemon` | `boolean` | Make use of the scdaemon tool. This option has the effect of enabling the ability to do smartcard operations. When disabled, this option passes disable-scdaemon setting to gpg-agent. |
| `services.gpg-agent.enableSshSupport` | `boolean` | Whether to use the GnuPG key agent for SSH keys. |
| `services.gpg-agent.enableZshIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Zsh integration. |
| `services.gpg-agent.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with “\n”` | Extra configuration lines to append to the gpg-agent configuration file. |
| `services.gpg-agent.grabKeyboardAndMouse` | `boolean` | Tell the pinentry to grab the keyboard and mouse. This option should in general be used to avoid X-sniffing attacks. When disabled, this option passes no-grab setting to gpg-agent. |
| `services.gpg-agent.maxCacheTtl` | `null or signed integer` | Set the maximum time a cache entry is valid to n seconds. After this time a cache entry will be expired even if it has been accessed recently or has been set using gpg-preset-passphrase. The default is 2 hours (7200 seconds). |
| `services.gpg-agent.maxCacheTtlSsh` | `null or signed integer` | Set the maximum time a cache entry used for SSH keys is valid to n seconds. After this time a cache entry will be expired even if it has been accessed recently or has been set using gpg-preset-passphrase. The default is 2 hours (7200 seconds). |
| `services.gpg-agent.noAllowExternalCache` | `boolean` | Tell Pinentry not to enable features which use an external cache for passphrases. |
| `services.gpg-agent.pinentry.package` | `null or package` | The pinentry-gnome3 package to use. Which pinentry interface to use. If not null , it sets pinentry-program in gpg-agent.conf . Beware that pinentry-gnome3 may not work on non-GNOME systems. You can fix it by adding the following to your configuration: |
| `services.gpg-agent.pinentry.program` | `null or string` | Which program to search for in the configured pinentry.package . |
| `services.gpg-agent.pinentryFlavor` | | |
| `services.gpg-agent.pinentryPackage` | | |
| `services.gpg-agent.sshKeys` | `null or (list of string)` | Which GPG keys (by keygrip) to expose as SSH keys. |
| `services.gpg-agent.verbose` | `boolean` | Whether to produce verbose output. |
